name: React 2
description: Evaluate your mastery of React and its concepts

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the useMemo hook?
options: Synchronizing a component with an external system
Triggering a re-render when a specific value changes
Memoizing the result of an expensive calculation
Managing complex state logic via a reducer function

questions.question: How does the useContext hook behave when a provider value updates?
options: It triggers a re-render for all consuming components
It updates the global state without re-rendering
It only updates components using the useReducer hook
It requires a manual page refresh to show changes

questions.question: What is the role of the 'key' prop in React lists?
options: To provide a unique identifier for CSS styling rules
To help React identify which items changed or moved
To ensure that all list items have a specific index
To allow direct access to the underlying DOM node

questions.question: What happens when you call a state setter function?
options: The current function execution is immediately stopped
The browser DOM is replaced by a new static string
The component is scheduled for a future re-render
The state object is mutated directly in the memory

questions.question: What is the main use case for the useRef hook?
options: Persisting values between renders without re-rendering
Storing data that must trigger a UI update on change
Fetching data from an API during the mounting phase
Defining a global context for the entire application

questions.question: Which statement about React Fragments is correct?
options: They add an extra <div> node to the final DOM tree
They are used to group elements without adding nodes
They prevent child components from being re-rendered
They are only available in class-based components

questions.question: What is a 'Higher-Order Component' (HOC)?
options: A component that renders another component as a child
A function that takes a component and returns a new one
A specialized hook for managing high-level application state
A method to increase the priority of a rendering task

questions.question: How do you handle errors in a React component tree?
options: Wrapping the entire component in a try-catch block
Using the useError hook to capture internal exceptions
Implementing an Error Boundary using class components
Setting a global variable to track the error status

questions.question: What is the purpose of the useCallback hook?
options: To cache a function definition between re-renders
To execute a function after the layout is painted
To transform a standard function into a React hook
To prevent a function from being called too many times

questions.question: Which hook is best for complex state transitions?
options: useState
useMemo
useContext
useReducer
